bins.name: Preserve Flexibility Over Rigid Rules
bins.desc: ATC consistently argues that flexibility is the most effective enabler of timely large-load interconnection. It supports a policy statement rather than binding rules, and asks that any requirements be narrowly tailored so transmission owners can continue using existing processes for conforming loads.
quotes: efficient and expeditious interconnection of large loads is no longer merely a commercial preference, but a matter of national security and an economic development imperative.
A "one-size-fits-all" approach could inadvertently hinder progress by upending established processes and increasing the risk of implementation delays.
ATC supports commenters recommending that the Commission consider adopting a policy statement or general guidance rather than a sweeping rule.
requirements should be narrowly tailored to focus on experienced issues and allow maximum flexibility.
the Commission should consider adopting threshold applicability requirements based upon specific operating characteristics.

Limiting applicability to the subset of loads that do not meet with certain operating criteria could provide adequate controls, ensure reliability, contain costs, while preserving the flexibility for transmission owners such as ATC to continue to interconnect traditional, conforming loads based upon our existing interconnection processes.

bins.name: Policy Statement Over Rulemaking
bins.desc: ATC favors Commission guidance or a policy statement over sweeping new rules, arguing that mandatory nationwide requirements would disrupt effective regional processes and invite litigation.
quotes: A "one-size-fits-all" approach could inadvertently hinder progress by upending established processes and increasing the risk of implementation delays.
ATC supports commenters recommending that the Commission consider adopting a policy statement or general guidance rather than a sweeping rule.

bins.name: Operating-Characteristic Thresholds
bins.desc: ATC recommends that any new requirements apply only to loads that fail specific operating criteria such as ramp rate or ride-through capability, limiting the rule to genuinely non-conforming loads.
quotes: requirements should be narrowly tailored to focus on experienced issues and allow maximum flexibility.
the Commission should consider adopting threshold applicability requirements based upon specific operating characteristics.

Limiting applicability to the subset of loads that do not meet with certain operating criteria could provide adequate controls, ensure reliability, contain costs, while preserving the flexibility for transmission owners such as ATC to continue to interconnect traditional, conforming loads based upon our existing interconnection processes.

bins.name: Separate Studies for Generation and Load
bins.desc: ATC opposes co-study of hybrid facilities as a default, citing functional differences between generation and load and reliability risks from misalignment at shared interconnection points. If co-study occurs, it requires safeguards and system protection facilities. Grid-enhancing technologies are useful but not substitutes for proper study and new transmission capacity.
quotes: ATC, along with other commenters, urges the Commission to separate studies for generation and load while preserving the flexibility for local planning authorities to rely upon their expertise to adjust sequencing and modeling.
Generation and load are functionally different and should be studied separately.
to the extent hybrid facilities are studied together, safeguards such as process matching and system protection facilities must be incorporated.
Misalignment between load and generation at a shared interconnection point can lead to unintended injections or withdrawals from the transmission system.
transmission owners lack the necessary tools to curtail firm service; therefore, the full impacts of any hybrid facility must be considered to prevent catastrophic consequences.

These tools can optimize existing assets, improve flexibility, and support localized stability, but they cannot fully mitigate reliability risks posed by hybrid facilities and large, highly variable loads.

bins.name: No Mandatory Joint Studies
bins.desc: ATC urges separate studies for generation and load, and argues that imposing coordinated studies nationwide would undermine reliability and effectiveness. Local planning authorities should retain discretion over study sequencing.
quotes: ATC, along with other commenters, urges the Commission to separate studies for generation and load while preserving the flexibility for local planning authorities to rely upon their expertise to adjust sequencing and modeling.
Generation and load are functionally different and should be studied separately.
to the extent hybrid facilities are studied together, safeguards such as process matching and system protection facilities must be incorporated.

bins.name: Hybrid Facility Reliability Safeguards
bins.desc: ATC acknowledges that hybrid facilities raise unique reliability risks from potential unintended injections or withdrawals, and that transmission owners lack tools to curtail firm service. Any framework for hybrid facilities must address these risks through process matching and system protection requirements.
quotes: ATC, along with other commenters, urges the Commission to separate studies for generation and load while preserving the flexibility for local planning authorities to rely upon their expertise to adjust sequencing and modeling.
to the extent hybrid facilities are studied together, safeguards such as process matching and system protection facilities must be incorporated.
Misalignment between load and generation at a shared interconnection point can lead to unintended injections or withdrawals from the transmission system.
transmission owners lack the necessary tools to curtail firm service; therefore, the full impacts of any hybrid facility must be considered to prevent catastrophic consequences.

bins.name: System Protection for Hybrid Facilities
bins.desc: ATC calls for mandatory system protection facilities in any co-study scenario, warning that the inability to curtail firm service means full reliability impacts must be analyzed to prevent catastrophic consequences.
quotes: to the extent hybrid facilities are studied together, safeguards such as process matching and system protection facilities must be incorporated.
Misalignment between load and generation at a shared interconnection point can lead to unintended injections or withdrawals from the transmission system.
transmission owners lack the necessary tools to curtail firm service; therefore, the full impacts of any hybrid facility must be considered to prevent catastrophic consequences.

bins.name: Rolled-In Cost Allocation, Not Direct Assignment
bins.desc: ATC opposes the ANOPR's 100% direct assignment proposal as overly complex and prone to disputes. It favors continued rolled-in allocation to all benefiting customers, accepts direct assignment only for facilities exclusively used by the new load, and insists that transmission owners retain a reasonable return on investment under any cost framework.
quotes: Limiting applicability to the subset of loads that do not meet with certain operating criteria could provide adequate controls, ensure reliability, contain costs, while preserving the flexibility for transmission owners such as ATC to continue to interconnect traditional, conforming loads based upon our existing interconnection processes.

ATC, along with several stakeholders, supports continued allocation of network upgrade costs to all transmission customers that benefit from the enhanced transmission system, consistent with Commission precedent.
ATC believes such direct assignment would be highly complex and likely result in disputes, confusion, and delays.
Direct assignment should be narrowly tailored to facilities exclusively attributable to and used by new loads to help contain costs and maintain alignment with cost-causation principles.
any future guidance, policy statement, or rulemaking should preserve transmission owners' ability to earn a reasonable return on transmission system investments, consistent with judicial precedent.

Transmission owners must continue to be compensated for the risks associated with owning, operating, and maintaining network upgrades through mechanisms such as initial funding under any future cost-assignment framework.

bins.name: Limit Direct Assignment of Upgrade Costs
bins.desc: ATC believes direct assignment of 100% of network upgrade costs to the load is too broad and complex. It should be confined to facilities exclusively attributable to the new load, and any framework must ensure TOs receive initial funding and a fair rate of return.
quotes: ATC, along with several stakeholders, supports continued allocation of network upgrade costs to all transmission customers that benefit from the enhanced transmission system, consistent with Commission precedent.
ATC believes such direct assignment would be highly complex and likely result in disputes, confusion, and delays.
Direct assignment should be narrowly tailored to facilities exclusively attributable to and used by new loads to help contain costs and maintain alignment with cost-causation principles.
any future guidance, policy statement, or rulemaking should preserve transmission owners' ability to earn a reasonable return on transmission system investments, consistent with judicial precedent.

Transmission owners must continue to be compensated for the risks associated with owning, operating, and maintaining network upgrades through mechanisms such as initial funding under any future cost-assignment framework.

bins.name: Large-Load Interconnection as National Priority
bins.desc: ATC frames timely interconnection of large loads as a matter of national security and economic development, using this framing to support arguments for flexibility and speed over regulatory complexity.
quotes: efficient and expeditious interconnection of large loads is no longer merely a commercial preference, but a matter of national security and an economic development imperative.

bins.name: GETs as Complements, Not Substitutes
bins.desc: ATC supports grid-enhancing technologies as useful operational tools but argues they cannot substitute for additional transmission capacity when dealing with large, highly variable loads.
quotes: These tools can optimize existing assets, improve flexibility, and support localized stability, but they cannot fully mitigate reliability risks posed by hybrid facilities and large, highly variable loads.

bins.name: Transmission Owner Rate of Return
bins.desc: ATC invokes long-standing judicial precedent (Hope Natural Gas, Bluefield Waterworks) to argue that any rulemaking must preserve transmission owners' ability to earn a reasonable return on network upgrade investments.
quotes: any future guidance, policy statement, or rulemaking should preserve transmission owners' ability to earn a reasonable return on transmission system investments, consistent with judicial precedent.
